definitions: A person or thing that skids.
A type of heavy vehicle used in logging to drag felled trees from the forest to a landing.
A type of footwear, often with non-slip soles, worn for comfort or safety.
examples: The skidders worked tirelessly to clear the fallen timber.
She put on her skidders to avoid slipping on the wet floor.
The new skidders improved efficiency at the logging site.
